formality.description: Swahili does not have a T-V pronoun distinction. 'Wewe' (you singular) is universal. Formality is expressed through vocabulary choice, use of honorifics (Bwana/Bibi), and register. The Swahili noun class system affects agreement patterns but does not encode formality.

gender.inclusiveGuidance: Swahili uses a noun class system rather than grammatical gender. Pronouns do not distinguish gender.

professional.label: Rasmi (Professional)
professional.description: Standard for software UI and business communication.
professional.prompt: Professional Swahili (Kiswahili sanifu). Modern standard Swahili appropriate for software interfaces and business communication. Clear, accessible vocabulary.
